title: A Trip to the South Library
article_text: A new reader at the library can help you learn the importance of reading. The guide for the south sector shows a short distance to the library. The government did not grant money for the new library, so we must use labor to build it. We must not make a mistake when we feed the fish in the library pond. The taxi driver waited for us, but he felt pain in his back. To ensure the project is good, we need an expert to check the surface of the wall. The chief of the library wants to satisfy every visitor with cool air and quiet rooms. The peace in the library is good, but the federal status of the building is not clear. A wave of new books will reveal a higher percentage of happy readers. I saw a big reader smile, and that made me feel happy.

sentences.zh: 图书馆的一位新读者可以帮助你学习阅读的重要性。

image_prompts: A person holding a book and smiling in a library, with shelves of books behind them.
A map showing a path from a 'south sector' label to a library building, with a short distance line.
A group of people using tools to build a library, with a sign that says 'No government grant'.
A person throwing food into a small pond near a library, with fish swimming.
A taxi driver sitting in a car, holding his back with a pained expression.
An expert in a hard hat checking the wall of a library with a measuring tool.
A library chief standing at the entrance, with a cool air conditioner and quiet signs inside.
A quiet library room with people reading, and a sign on the wall that says 'Federal Status Pending'.
A wave of colorful books flying toward a library, with happy readers standing outside.
A big reader smiling widely in a library, with another person looking happy nearby.
